evals.prompt: Generate a specification.yaml from the approved design document. The design document describes a user authentication module with JWT-based authentication. Key requirements: 1) Users can login with correct credentials, 2) Login fails with wrong credentials, 3) Token refresh mechanism. Generate the specification with REQ-AUTH IDs, AC IDs in Gherkin format, and design decisions about JWT usage.
evals.expected_output: A valid specification.yaml file with: specification.id (SPEC-AUTH-XXX), at least 3 requirements (REQ-AUTH-001/002/003), each requirement has acceptance_criteria with given/when/then, and design_decisions with rationale

evals.prompt: Generate specification.yaml for an API module that handles CRUD operations for user profiles. Requirements: 1) GET /api/users returns list of users, 2) POST /api/users creates new user, 3) PUT /api/users/:id updates user, 4) DELETE /api/users/:id deletes user. Include API contracts with request/response schemas, edge cases for pagination and authentication.
evals.expected_output: A valid specification.yaml with: api_contracts section with 4 endpoints, each endpoint has request/response defined, edge_cases for pagination limits, security_considerations for authentication

evals.prompt: Generate specification.yaml and validate that all IDs follow the correct format: SPEC-XXX-XXX for specification.id, REQ-XXX-XXX for requirements.id, AC-XXX-XXX-XX for acceptance_criteria.id, DD-XXX-XXX for design_decisions.id. The module is a notification system with requirements: 1) Send email notifications, 2) Send push notifications, 3) Track notification status.
evals.expected_output: A specification.yaml where: all ID formats match regex patterns (SPEC-[A-Z]+-[0-9]+, REQ-[A-Z]+-[0-9]+, AC-[A-Z]+-[0-9]+-[0-9]+), all acceptance_criteria have given/when/then fields with minimum 5 characters each
